summary:
**Fund Activity**
CD&R closed its twelfth flagship fund at $10 billion, significantly exceeding its $8B target. The fund's successful raise signals continued LP confidence in operationally-focused PE strategies and suggests an upcoming wave of new platform investments. Meanwhile, Gemspring Capital expanded its technology-enabled services portfolio with the acquisition of DataSync Solutions.

**Personnel Moves**
Keystone Capital made a notable hire, bringing on former Bain partner Sarah Mitchell as Operating Partner to lead healthcare investments. This signals Keystone's intent to deepen its healthcare vertical—a space where AI/ML value creation is increasingly relevant. At CD&R, operating partner Raj Patel joined the board of Cornerstone Building Brands, reinforcing the firm's hands-on governance approach.

**Portfolio Highlights**
CIVC Partners portfolio company Allegient posted impressive 40% year-over-year revenue growth, driven by expansion into healthcare administration services. The strong performance may position Allegient as a near-term platform for add-on acquisitions.

**Actionable Opportunities**
Three items warrant team attention this morning: (1) CD&R's new $10B fund will begin deploying capital—this is a prime window to pitch tech due diligence capabilities for their acquisition pipeline. (2) Keystone's new healthcare operating partner likely means expanded deal flow in that sector. (3) NEP is exploring a sale of its industrial coatings platform at $500M+—buyer-side operational due diligence may be needed.
